title: CI: use different TMPDIR on prior-fedora
body:
A small number of tests are assuming that TMPDIR == /tmp. These tests fail when that assumption does not hold.

Set TMPDIR=/var/tmp on prior-fedora, as a way to catch those. /dev/shm would be a slightly better choice, because the string "tmp" does not appear it in, but it's way too small to be of any use: it fills up in the e2e prefetch.

This PR exposed a nasty bug in our Makefile: using "TMPDIR" as temporary variable completely unrelated to (and inconsistent with) the actual established use of TMPDIR. Solution: rename that variable and make it lower case. Do the same with two other ALL-CAP variables.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This is going to fail CI, because #19267 has not merged.

Double yay:
* quadlet test failed, as expected (indicating that this PR does what I hoped it would do)
* no other tests failed (aside from flakes) (indicating that no other tests are TMPDIR-broken)

Still not ready to merge, because of the scary Makefile changes. I will clean those up tomorrow.

You PR description says: `TMPDIR=/dev/shm` while the commit and code uses `/var/tmp`.
Did you try /dev/shm first and that failed?

Also did you check the test speed? If it no longer uses tmpfs it may be slower.

`/dev/shm` was my first try. It failed (because of the broken Makefile:TMPDIR stuff, but I didn't know that).  I updated to `/var/tmp`, changed it in the git commit message, but not in Cirrus because WIP. Only after that did I discover the broken Makefile.

Tmpfs: not a factor. I did a Rerun-in-terminal and noticed that `/tmp` is not tmpfs, it's just a subdir of root. `/dev/shm` is a 2G tmpfs. I'm undecided as to whether to try to go back to it, if that will be enough space for e2e tests.

Speed: `cirrus-pr-timing` actually shows a very slight speedup, O(1m), but that's well within normal variation so I consider it an artifact:

| type | distro              | user     | DB     |    local |   remote | container |
| ---- | --------            | ----     | --     |    ----: |   -----: | --------: |
| 17831 (baseline) |
| int  | fedora-37           | root     | sqlite |    38:27 |    39:57 |     38:31 |
| int  | fedora-37           | rootless | sqlite |    34:34 |          |
| sys  | fedora-37           | root     | sqlite |    36:24 |    22:29 |
| sys  | fedora-37           | rootless | sqlite |    33:35 |          |
| 19281 (this PR) |
| int  | fedora-37           | root     |        |    37:29 |    38:13 |     37:48 |
| int  | fedora-37           | rootless |        |    32:46 |          |
| sys  | fedora-37           | root     |        |   !34:01 |    21:20 |
| sys  | fedora-37           | rootless |        |    31:44 |          |
@containers/podman-maintainers could we get this in? The bogus-TMPDIR bug led to wasted energy on #19351, and will no doubt cause other future problems.
